subject: Saturday Practice Schedule Change
body:
Hi Parents,

Just a quick note that this Saturday's practice for the U10 team has been moved to 10:00 AM instead of the usual 9:00 AM. We'll still be at Lincoln Park Field 3 and practice will run until 11:30 AM.

Please make sure your kids bring extra water as it's supposed to be warm.

Thanks,
Coach Smith

subject: Science Fair Project Due Date
body:
Dear Parents,

This is a reminder that the Science Fair projects are due on March 28th. Students should bring their completed projects to the gymnasium by 8:30 AM.

The Science Fair viewing for parents will be that evening from 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM in the school gymnasium.

Please let me know if you have any questions.

Best,
Mrs. Johnson
3rd Grade Teacher

subject: Spring Recital Information
body:
Dear Dance Families,

We're excited to announce our Spring Recital! Details:

Date: April 12th
Time: 2:00 PM
Location: Downtown Performing Arts Center

Dancers should arrive by 1:00 PM for warm-up. Costumes will be distributed next week.

Tickets are $15 per person and can be purchased at the front desk.

See you there!

subject: March Newsletter - Lincoln Elementary
body:
Lincoln Elementary PTA Monthly Newsletter

Hello families! Here's what's happening this month:

- Book fair proceeds totaled $2,500 - thank you!
- New playground equipment arriving in April
- Volunteer appreciation week is coming up
- Follow us on social media for updates

Thank you for your continued support!

subject: Important: Game location change this weekend
body:
Hi team parents,

Due to field maintenance, our game this Saturday has been moved to Riverside Park instead of our usual home field. Game time remains 1:00 PM.

Riverside Park address: 456 River Road

Please arrive 30 minutes early for warm-up.

Go team!
Coach Smith

subject: Rescheduling Alice's lesson
body:
Hi,

I need to reschedule Alice's piano lesson this week. Can we move it to Thursday instead? I have openings at 3:30 or 4:00.

Let me know what works best.

Thanks,
Ms. Chen

subject: Book Report Due Date - 3rd Grade
body:
Dear Parents,

This is a reminder that the 3rd grade book reports are due on Friday, March 21st. Students should turn in their completed reports to their homeroom teacher at the start of the school day.

The report should be at least 2 pages and include a summary and personal reflection.

Please reach out if your child needs extra time.

Best,
Mrs. Davis

subject: Permission Slip - Nature Center Field Trip
body:
Dear Parents,

We are planning a field trip to the Greenfield Nature Center on April 3rd for all 3rd graders. The signed permission slip is due back by March 25th.

We will send bus departure and return times once they are confirmed with the transportation office. Students should bring a packed lunch.

Please sign and return the attached form to your child's teacher.

Thank you,
Lincoln Elementary Office

subject: 4th Grade Weekly Update - Read-a-thon & Essay Contest
body:
Dear Families,

Our class has done such a great job with the Read-a-thon, let's keep this reading up well after this week.

It's the time of year for the annual Margaret Thompson Essay Contest sponsored jointly by the Los Altos History Museum and the LA Historical Commission. This is the 42nd year of this community essay contest.

Please talk to your child about this great opportunity! If you would like a hard copy of instructions please ask your child to grab one from me. Feel free to email with any questions!

This week in 4th grade:

This week in reading:
We began our Project Based Learning nonfiction unit: Ready for Anything, in which students will become experts in one natural disaster (hazard). This week students were researchers, exploring and reading articles from different resources while taking column notes to support their project.
We continue on our second read aloud Holes.
We continue with First Chapter Friday- where I read a chapter of a book each week to expose students to new genres and different books to encourage reading!

This week in writing:
Students continue to work on their argument writing essays focusing this week on adding examples for each of their reasons.

This week in math:
Students are learning about multiplying fractions and conceptually showing fraction understanding.

This week in social studies:
Students are learning briefly about the Mexican American War and we begin studying about the Gold Rush.

This week in science:
Students continue using stream tables to add different variables and how it would change their outcome.

Important Dates:
2/16-2/20 - Winter Break

Links:
4th grade photo album
Scholastic Book Room Link
Sign up for a free Fourth Grade Adventure Pass

subject: Swim Team Tryouts + Registration Deadline
body:
Dear Families,

Swim team tryouts for the spring season will be held on Saturday, April 5th from 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM at the Community Rec Center Pool.

Registration forms and the $40 fee are due by March 28th. Late registrations will not be accepted.

Please bring goggles, a swim cap, and a towel to tryouts.

See you at the pool!
Coach Rivera

subject: Reminder: Art Portfolio Submission
body:
Hello Parents,

Students need to submit their completed art portfolios by April 1st. Portfolios should contain at least 5 pieces from this semester.

The spring art show will be on April 10th from 5:30 PM to 7:30 PM in the school cafeteria. All families are welcome to attend!

Ms. Park
Art Teacher

subject: SwimFit Blue 2 - Practice Time Change
body:
SwimFit Blue 2 Families,

Practice Time on Monday, February 9, 2026 has been changed to 7:00-7:45 PM at JLS Middle School. This update has been posted to the Monthly Calendar.

Thank you & Go Alto!
